systems.label: Age
systems.summary: Global time-period definitions that set world-wide economic parameters, military scale modifiers, and hegemon availability for each historical era.

systems.label: AI Diplomatic Chance
systems.summary: Weighted-score tables the AI uses to evaluate acceptance of diplomatic actions such as marriage proposals, subject demands, and peace offers.

systems.label: Alert Descriptions
systems.summary: Maps named alert IDs to their UI presentation properties (icon, priority, tooltip title); contains no trigger logic.

systems.label: Artist Types
systems.summary: Named artist specializations (painter, composer, calligrapher, etc.) with optional potential triggers restricting them to certain religions or advances.

systems.label: Artist Work
systems.summary: Defines categories of cultural artifact artists can create and the country/location modifiers they apply, cross-referencing artist_types.

systems.label: Attribute Columns
systems.summary: Configures visible columns and sort options for every interactive selection list in the game (country picker, character picker, etc.).

systems.label: Avatars
systems.summary: Divine manifestations in the Hindu religion system that countries can adopt to receive ongoing modifiers and triggered effects.

systems.label: Advances
systems.summary: The research tree of EU5 — discrete nodes a country researches to unlock content, gain modifiers, and gate buildings, laws, CBs, and reforms behind progression.

systems.label: Auto Modifiers
systems.summary: Persistent modifiers applied automatically to a scoped entity whenever their conditions are met, translating game state into ongoing mechanical effects.

systems.label: Buildings
systems.summary: Location-level constructs that employ pops, consume goods, and apply modifiers; defined in building_types/ and building_categories/.

systems.label: Casus Belli
systems.summary: Legal justifications a country may use to declare war, binding declaration logic to a war goal type and governing valid province targets.

systems.label: Character Interactions
systems.summary: Scripted button-driven actions players use to manage individual characters — executing, pardoning, appointing, marrying, promoting, or commissioning art.

systems.label: Child Educations
systems.summary: Educational programs for child characters at court that apply character modifiers and determine which adult traits emerge at coming-of-age.

systems.label: Cultures
systems.summary: Ethnic and linguistic identities of pops, characters, and locations; each culture sets a language, map color, opinion relationships, and optional modifiers.

systems.label: Disasters
systems.summary: Country-level crisis events that apply ongoing penalties and fire monthly events while active; only one can be active per country at a time.

systems.label: Government Reforms
systems.summary: Optional modifiers and mechanics a country can adopt on top of its base government type; multiple reforms can be held simultaneously.

systems.label: Government Types
systems.summary: Top-level classification of a country's ruling system, determining government power resource, heir selection methods, and available laws and reforms.

systems.label: Laws
systems.summary: Named policy slots each holding one active policy; represent broad policy areas where switching costs a price and takes implementation time.

systems.label: Modifiers
systems.summary: Umbrella guide to EU5's modifier systems: inline, auto, scripted, and static — clarifies the four distinct systems and where to find documentation.

systems.label: Policies
systems.summary: In v1.1.10 the policies/ folder is empty by design — policies are created indirectly from laws, which carry all policy-equivalent content.

systems.label: Production Methods
systems.summary: Goods consumption and production recipes for buildings, defining inputs and outputs as either named PM definitions or inline building blocks.

systems.label: Religions
systems.summary: Faith definitions assigning each religion to a group, declaring its mechanics (karma, purity, honor), definition modifiers, opinion relationships, and sub-system registrations.

systems.label: Religious Aspects
systems.summary: Optional doctrines or practices countries can adopt for their religion, applying modifier blocks and opinion effects with other aspects.

systems.label: Holy Sites
systems.summary: Location-anchored sacred sites for one or more religions, with importance-scaled modifiers applied to the location, owning country, and matching religion.

systems.label: Religious Focuses
systems.summary: Religion-specific research projects a country completes over time to earn permanent modifiers, assigned to religions via their definition blocks.

systems.label: Religious Schools
systems.summary: Three religion sub-systems: schools (philosophical traditions granting modifiers), figures (named character roles per faith), and factions (court power blocs).

systems.label: Gods
systems.summary: Religion-specific deities countries can adopt for modifiers and triggered effects, associated with religion groups or individual religions.

systems.label: Scripted Effects
systems.summary: Named, reusable effect blocks callable anywhere in script with optional parameterized arguments, eliminating repetition for complex effect logic.

systems.label: Scripted Triggers
systems.summary: Named, reusable trigger blocks callable anywhere in script with optional parameterized arguments, mirroring the scripted_effects pattern.

systems.label: Subject Types
systems.summary: Defines every overlord–subject relationship (vassal, march, tributary, dominion, etc.) including war-joining, annexation, modifiers, and AI weighting.

systems.label: Technologies
systems.summary: Not applicable in v1.1.10 — the technologies/ folder is absent; research progression is handled by the advances system.

systems.label: Traits
systems.summary: Character-level modifiers shaping ruler, general, admiral, artist, and explorer effectiveness, with allow triggers, MTTH chance, and societal value interactions.

systems.label: War Goals
systems.summary: Defines what a war is fought for and what can be demanded at the peace table, referenced by casus belli via war_goal_type.

systems.label: Country Interactions
systems.summary: Scripted diplomatic or subject-management actions one country performs toward another, covering alliances, religion enforcement, and tribute demands.

systems.label: Generic Actions
systems.summary: Fully-scripted action system covering own-country, diplomatic, character, location, IO, and parliament actions without requiring code-side handlers.

systems.label: International Organizations
systems.summary: Multi-country entities above bilateral diplomacy (HRE, Papacy, leagues, alliances) with membership rules, leader modifiers, parliaments, payments, and special statuses.

systems.label: Estates
systems.summary: Political factions within a country (nobles, clergy, burghers, etc.) with power, satisfaction, and opinion; estate privileges grant modifier blocks while active.

systems.label: Goods
systems.summary: Tradeable commodities with base price, extraction method, food value, and per-pop demand; extended by prices/, goods_demand/, and goods_demand_category/ folders.

systems.label: Pop Types
systems.summary: Distinct population classes (nobles, clergy, burghers, laborers, soldiers, peasants, tribesmen, slaves) with food consumption, promotion rates, and estate assignments.

systems.label: Employment Systems
systems.summary: Determines building staffing priority under labour scarcity, grants a country modifier while active, and nudges societal values toward specific orientations.

systems.label: Societal Values
systems.summary: Bidirectional political/economic/cultural sliders where both ends apply blended modifiers proportional to the country's current position on each axis.

systems.label: Heir Selections
systems.summary: Scripted succession law rules defining candidate filtering, scoring, and validation for determining the next ruler.

systems.label: Missions
systems.summary: Country objective chains with weighted selection from a potential pool; each mission contains a DAG of tasks with prerequisites, timed completion, and reward effects.

systems.label: Unit Types
systems.summary: Defines military and naval units with category-inherited base stats, terrain modifiers, and copy_from inheritance; unit abilities define scripted field actions.

systems.label: AI Biases
systems.summary: Named opinion, trust, and antagonism modifier entries applied between countries by engine code or scripted effects, with optional caps, expiry, and yearly decay.

systems.label: AI Rivals
systems.summary: Tag-specific triggers and block lists that restrict which countries the AI will choose as rivals; players are unaffected.

systems.label: AI Generic Action Lists
systems.summary: Named conditional sets of generic actions the AI evaluates periodically; potential trigger gates the list, actions block lists candidates.

systems.label: AI War (Join War Rules)
systems.summary: Named triggers that block AI countries from joining specific wars as allies; rule key doubles as localisation for the decline reason.

systems.label: Scripted Relations
systems.summary: Defines all bilateral diplomatic relationships (alliance, guarantee, access, embargo, etc.) with directionality, war behaviour, monthly flows, AI weights, and lifecycle effects.

systems.label: Scripted Country Names
systems.summary: Conditional country name overrides triggered by simultaneous country, capital, and territory checks; used for colonial naming (New Spain, Jamaica, etc.).

systems.label: Scripted GUIs
systems.summary: Script-driven GUI button definitions with show/valid/effect/AI fields; no vanilla definitions in v1.1.10.

systems.label: Scriptable Hints
systems.summary: Context-sensitive advisor hints shown when priority triggers fire, with per-playstyle localisation tag variants; UI-only, no gameplay effects.

systems.label: Effect Localization
systems.summary: Maps effect names to tooltip localisation keys with pronoun (global/first/third) and tense (present/past) variants; no gameplay logic.

systems.label: Trigger Localization
systems.summary: Maps trigger names to tooltip localisation keys including pronoun variants, NOT forms, value-comparison operator specialisations, and any-trigger count/percent variants.

systems.label: Customizable Localization
systems.summary: Named inline loc functions called from localisation strings via [FunctionKey()]; return the first matching loc key from a trigger-gated cascade with a fallback.

systems.label: Script Values
systems.summary: Named constants and scoped math formulas referenceable anywhere a numeric argument is accepted; support arithmetic, conditionals, randomness, scope traversal, and list aggregation.

systems.label: On Actions
systems.summary: Named engine-fired hooks that additively accumulate event lists, on-action chains, and inline effects across all files; the primary safe injection point for mods.

systems.label: Scripted Geography
systems.summary: Named groupings of locations, areas, regions, and province definitions that expose iteration lists, membership tests, and country presence checks.

systems.label: Scripted Diplomatic Objectives
systems.summary: Named AI behaviour templates that instruct a country to persistently pursue a diplomatic strategy (improve, antagonise, destroy) toward a filtered set of target countries.

systems.label: Scripted Modifiers
systems.summary: Named reusable weight-modifier blocks for random_list and AI weighting; no vanilla definitions in v1.1.10.

systems.label: Static Modifiers
systems.summary: Named modifier blocks applied explicitly to scoped entities by engine code or scripted effects, with optional decay and auto-removal on religion/culture change.

systems.label: Scripted Lists
systems.summary: Named, filtered views of built-in scope iteration lists; define one block and the engine registers every_/any_/random_/ordered_ variants automatically.

systems.label: Scripted Rules
systems.summary: Reserved system for named boolean rule overrides; folder exists but contains no vanilla definitions in v1.1.10.

systems.label: Languages
systems.summary: Name pools for characters and dynasties, associated with language families and referenced by cultures; interact with court, market, and common language mechanics.

systems.label: Levies
systems.summary: Pop-based conscription system granting units from eligible pop types; file order determines priority — specific levies must precede generic ones. Includes recruitment_method definitions for training speed and experience.

systems.label: Situations
systems.summary: Global multi-country historical crises (Black Death, Reformation, Hundred Years' War, Sengoku, ~20 total) with monthly callbacks, optional resolution votes, and map overlays.

systems.label: Institutions
systems.summary: Age-linked systems (Feudalism, Printing Press, etc.) that spread location-by-location via trade and borders; a country embraces when enough of its locations have adopted the institution.

systems.label: Diseases
systems.summary: Epidemic outbreaks using an SIR-like model; 7 vanilla diseases (plague, influenza, malaria, measles, smallpox, typhus, great pestilence) spread location-by-location with configurable R0, mortality, and map overlays.

systems.label: Parliament
systems.summary: Three-layer legislative/estate negotiation system: parliament types (structural form), agendas (periodic estate demands), and issues (policy motions with debate and vote mechanics).

systems.label: Country Ranks
systems.summary: Prestige tiers (duchy, kingdom, empire, etc.) evaluated dynamically; each rank applies a modifier block and sets diplomatic capacity, fort limits, and AI interaction range.

systems.label: Formable Countries
systems.summary: Scripted nation-forming decisions requiring territory thresholds, a plausibility rule (historical/plausible/fantasy), and a country rank level; form_effect fires on transformation.

systems.label: Hegemons
systems.summary: World-power designations (economic, naval, military, diplomatic, cultural) auto-awarded to the leading great power in each domain; each status grants a unique modifier block.

systems.label: Regencies
systems.summary: Interregnum and regency definitions for periods without an adult ruler; specifies eligible regents, start/end effects, modifiers, and whether assignment is engine-internal.

systems.label: Cabinet Actions
systems.summary: Ongoing minister assignments (adm/dip/mil) that continuously apply country and location modifiers while active; share the select_trigger pipeline with country_interactions.

systems.label: Resolutions
systems.summary: Proposal-and-vote system for International Organizations and Situations; supports unilateral or voted enactment, weighted voting, deadlines, and multi-stage target selection.

systems.label: Subject Military Stances
systems.summary: Named AI priority weight sets governing subject military behaviour — pursuit, defence, sieging, logistics, and recruitment — assignable per subject type or country.

systems.label: Unit Formation Preference
systems.summary: Named battle formation templates defining weighted unit-type distributions for left, centre, right, and reserve wings with per-wing max frontage multipliers.

systems.label: Road Types
systems.summary: Infrastructure upgrade tiers (gravel → railroad) each defining movement speed, market access, capital proximity, build cost, and construction goods requirements.

systems.label: Location Ranks
systems.summary: Settlement upgrade tiers (village → town → city) applying rank modifiers to locations and per-city modifiers to owning countries; upgrades are gated by population and current rank.

systems.label: Town Setups
systems.summary: Named presets of starting building configurations applied to locations at scenario start; used by setup files to give historically appropriate initial infrastructure.

systems.label: Historical Scores
systems.summary: Reference leaderboard entries (famous rulers/moments with tag and score) shown on the end-game score screen for contextual comparison; display-only, no gameplay effect.

systems.label: Map Geography (Climates, Topography, Vegetation)
systems.summary: Three stacking location property systems: climates (winter/population/disease), topography (terrain shape/movement/combat), and vegetation (ground cover/supply/food); all three location_modifier blocks sum additively.

systems.label: Genes & Persistent DNA
systems.summary: Heritable portrait gene definitions (colour, morphology, accessories) and per-character DNA overrides for named historical figures; consumed by the portrait rendering engine.

systems.label: Ethnicities
systems.summary: Probability distributions over gene expression ranges for regional/cultural character groups; cultures reference ethnicity definitions to determine what their characters look like.

systems.label: Death Reasons, Heir Reasons & Country Description Categories
systems.summary: Three small UI enumerations: death causes with optional contextual parameters, heir designation reasons, and country lobby profile category labels; display-only with localization key conventions.
